member.name: P:SimpleMem.Exceptions.ErrorCodes.CodeLookup
member.summary: Lookup dictionary for relevant error codes and their meanings.

member.name: T:SimpleMem.Exceptions.MemoryReadException
member.summary: Thrown when memory read operations have failed. Intended to be used as a support tool for hard-to-debug issues.

member.name: T:SimpleMem.Exceptions.MemoryWriteException
member.summary: Thrown when memory write operations have failed. Intended to be used as a support tool for hard-to-debug issues.

member.name: T:SimpleMem.Extensions.MemoryExtensions
member.summary: Extensions for Memory

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Extensions.MemoryExtensions.StaticOffset(SimpleMem.Memory,System.Int32)
member.summary: Extension that serves as a wrapper for mem.ModuleBaseAddress + offset. Useful for storing lots of static offsets that derive directly from the ModuleBaseAddress (these are not the same as pointers, although they are similar).
member.param: The memory instance in which the base address exists
Any address

member.name: T:SimpleMem.Extensions.PointerExtensions
member.summary: Extensions for MultiLevelPtr

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Extensions.PointerExtensions.ReadValue``1(SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr,SimpleMem.Memory)
member.summary: Reads the value, in memory, of this . This extension saves a separate call to .
member.param: The to read the value from
The instance in which this value lays

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Extensions.PointerExtensions.WriteValue``1(SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr,SimpleMem.Memory,``0)
member.summary: Writes the value in the same fashion as

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Extensions.PointerExtensions.WriteBytes(SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr,SimpleMem.Memory,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Writes the bytes to memory at the address resolved from the MultiLevelPtr.

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Extensions.PointerExtensions.GetAddress(SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr,SimpleMem.Memory)
member.summary: Gets the address resolved from the given mlPtr.
member.param: The to read the address from
The instance in which this address lays
member.returns: A IntPtr containing the address, if found.

member.name: M:SimpleMem.HelperFunctions.WriteProcessMemory(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.Byte*,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Wrapper for kernel32.dll WriteProcessMemory. Calls VirtualProtectEx() on memory to allow writing to protected memory.
member.param: Whether the memory address space to write to has been vetted by a VirtualProtectEx call
member.exception: Thrown if the memory failed to be written

member.name: M:SimpleMem.HelperFunctions.ReadProcessMemory(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.Byte*,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Wrapper for kernel32.dll ReadProcessMemory
member.param: Whether the memory access is processed by a VirtualProtectEx call

member.name: M:SimpleMem.HelperFunctions.GetProcess(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the process (if possible) based on the class's processName.
member.exception: Thrown if the process is not found.

member.name: M:SimpleMem.HelperFunctions.GetProcessList
member.summary: Returns a list of all currently running executables' process names (regardless of architecture). The names of these processes can be used as the processName constructor argument.

member.name: T:SimpleMem.Memory
member.summary: Class for cross-architecture memory manipulation.

member.name: P:SimpleMem.Memory.ProcessAccessLevel
member.summary: The user-defined desired access level for which the process was opened under.

member.name: P:SimpleMem.Memory.Process
member.summary: The current process

member.name: P:SimpleMem.Memory.ProcessHandle
member.summary: Pointer to the handle of the opened process in memory.

member.name: P:SimpleMem.Memory.PtrSize
member.summary: The size of pointers for this process.

member.name: P:SimpleMem.Memory.Module
member.summary: Module assosciated with the provided moduleName, or the process's MainModule by default.

member.name: P:SimpleMem.Memory.ModuleBaseAddress
member.summary: Base address of the module

member.name: P:SimpleMem.Memory.ModuleBaseAddressHex
member.summary: ModuleBaseAddress in hex form. Casting ModuleBaseAddress .ToString() directly returns a base-10 number.

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Memory.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Int64)
member.summary: Opens a handle to the given processName at the provided moduleName. For example, if I have a process named "FTLGame" and my desired base address is located at "FTLGame.exe" + 0xABCD..., the processName would be "FTLGame" and the moduleName would be "FTLGame.exe".
member.param: The name of the process. Use and find your process name if unsure. That value can be passed in as this parameter.
The name of the module to use as the base pointer. This defaults to your application's executable if left null.

The desired access level. The minimum required for reading is AccessLevel.READ and the minimum required for writing is AccessLevel.WRITE | AccessLevel.OPERATION. AccessLevel.ALL_ACCESS gives full read-write access to the process.

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Memory.AoBScan(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Array of Byte pattern scan. Allows scanning for an exact array of bytes with wildcard support. Note: Partial wildcards are not supported and will be converted into full wildcards. This has a small possibility of resulting in more matches than desired. (e.g. AB ?1 turns into AB ??)
member.param: The pattern of bytes to look for. Bytes are separated by spaces. Wildcards (?? symbols) are supported.
Whether to abort the scan after the first match.

example.code: var addresses = AoBScan("03 AD FF ?? ?? ?? 4D"); // Returns a list of addresses found (if any) matching the pattern.

member.exception: Thrown if marked as once but no matches found.

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Memory.WriteMemory(System.IntPtr,System.Byte[],System.Boolean)
member.summary: Writes the given byte array directly to memory at lpBaseAddress
member.param: The address to overwrite data at
The bytes to write
Whether the memory access is processed by a VirtualProtectEx call. This can resolve some memory access violation errors at a significant speed cost - use sparingly.

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Memory.WriteMemory``1(System.IntPtr,``0)
member.summary: Overwrites the value at lpBaseAddress with the provided value.
member.param: The address in memory to write to
The value to write

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Memory.WriteMemory(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Text.Encoding)
member.summary: Writes a string to the given address. If the length of value is longer than the maximum length of the string supported by the process at lpBaseAddress, the contents of value will overflow into subsequent addresses and will be truncated by .
member.param: The address in memory to write to
The string to write
Whether the written string should be null terminated. If false, the value written will overwrite chars beginning from lpBaseAddress through the end of value. See the example for more info.
The encoding to write the string in. UTF-8 by default.

members.member: This overload can possibly resolve some memory access violation errors at a significant speed cost. Use sparingly.

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Memory.ReadMemory``1(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Reads memory of the desired type from lpBaseAddress.
member.param: The address to read from
Whether the memory access is processed by a VirtualProtectEx call. This can resolve some memory access violation errors at a significant speed cost - use sparingly.
member.typeparam: The type of data to read
member.exception: Type specified could not be read

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Memory.ReadMemory(System.IntPtr,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Read memory from lpBaseAddress into the given buffer. The amount of memory read (in bytes) equates to the length of the buffer.
member.param: The address to read from
The buffer to fill with read data
Whether the memory access is processed by a VirtualProtectEx call. This can resolve some memory access violation errors at a significant speed cost - use sparingly.

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Memory.ReadMemory(System.IntPtr,System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Read memory from lpBaseAddress into the given buffer. The amount of memory read (in bytes) equates to the length of the buffer.
member.param: The address to read from
The buffer to fill with read data
Whether the memory access is processed by a VirtualProtectEx call. This can resolve some memory access violation errors at a significant speed cost - use sparingly.

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Memory.ReadString(System.IntPtr,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Text.Encoding)
member.summary: Reads the specified number of bytes from lpBaseAddress in the specified encoding. The string is expected to start at the provided address. The user is not expected to know the length of the string obtained.
member.param: The address to read from
The maximum size of the string in bytes

Whether or not to return the first null-terminated string found. If false, returns a string containing the first (size) bytes beginning from lpBaseAddress. For example, if false and size is 255, returns a string with 255 bytes of character data beginning from lpBaseAddress.
The encoding to process the read string through. UTF-8 by default.
member.returns: The first string read from lpBaseAddress in UTF-8 encoding, unless otherwise specified.
member.exception: A string could not be read from the address.

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Memory.AoBScanFirst(System.String)
member.summary: Array of Byte pattern scan. Allows scanning for an exact array of bytes with wildcard support. Note: Partial wildcards are not supported and will be converted into full wildcards. This has a small possibility of resulting in more matches than desired. (e.g. AB ?1 turns into AB ??). This overload of AoBScan returns the first address found. A MemoryReadException is thrown if no matches are found for the byte scan. This is faster than getting a complete AoBScan and then filtering that list.
member.param: The pattern of bytes to look for. Bytes are separated by spaces. Wildcards (?? symbols) are supported.

example.code: var addresses = AoBScan("03 AD FF ?? ?? ?? 4D"); // Returns a list of addresses found (if any) matching the pattern.

member.exception: Thrown if marked as once but no matches found.
member.returns: The first match found in the byte scan.

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Memory.ReadAddressFromMlPtr(SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr)
M:SimpleMem.Memory.ReadValueFromMlPtr``1(SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr)
member.summary: Resolves the value from the address found from mlPtr
member.param: The MultiLevelPtr to read from
member.typeparam: The type of data to read from the address resolved from the MultiLevelPtr.
member.returns: Value found from the resolved MultiLevelPtr

member.name: M:SimpleMem.Memory.ReadStringFromMlPtr(SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Text.Encoding)
member.summary: Reads a string from the address found from mlPtr.
member.param: The MultiLevelPtr to read from
The maximum size of the string in bytes

Whether or not to return the first null-terminated string found. If false, returns a string containing the first (size) bytes beginning from lpBaseAddress. For example, if false and size is 255, returns a string with 255 bytes of character data beginning from lpBaseAddress.
The encoding to process the read string through. UTF-8 by default.
member.returns: The first string read from lpBaseAddress in UTF-8 encoding, unless otherwise specified.

member.name: T:SimpleMem.Models.ACCESS_LEVEL
member.summary: Access level to open a process with

member.name: T:SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr
member.summary:
Class for conveniently definining multi-level pointers. Multi-level pointers are used for obtaining values even after restarts of programs (unlike a single, non-static memory address).
Consider a game called "MyGame" with a module of "MyGame.exe". "MyGame.exe" is given a memory address that changes with each launch of the program, but all of the values desired (such as gold, points, exp, etc.) lay the same distance away in memory from the module. Say the offset for gold is 0xC and exp is 0xD. A MultiLevelPtr can be created from the "base address" of the module with the offsets being 0xC for gold and 0xD for exp. Assuming the base address and offsets are correct, the desired values will always be returned.

member.name: P:SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr.Base
member.summary: Base address of the pointer chain

member.name: P:SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr.Offsets
member.summary: Optional list of offsets containing pointer offsets (from the provided base).

member.name: M:SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr.#ctor(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr[])
member.param: The address the pointer starts from. This is almost always the ModuleBaseAddress.
The offsets needed to decipher the chain

member.name: M:SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr.#ctor(System.IntPtr,System.Int32[])
member.param: The address the pointer starts from. This is almost always the ModuleBaseAddress.
The offsets needed to decipher the chain

member.name: M:SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr.#ctor(SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr,System.Int32[])
member.summary: Creates a multi level pointer from an existing one, then adds the provided offsets to the old baseMlPtr's offsets list.
member.param: The previous MultiLevelPtr to base this one from
Collection of offsets to append to the old base offsets

member.name: M:SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr.#ctor(SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr,System.Int64[])
member.summary: Creates a multi level pointer from an existing one, then adds the provided offsets to the old baseMlPtr's offsets list.
member.param: The previous MultiLevelPtr to base this one from
Collection of offsets to append to the old base offsets

member.name: M:SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr.#ctor(System.Int64,System.Int32[])
member.param: The address the pointer starts from. This is almost always the ModuleBaseAddress.
The offsets needed to decipher the chain

member.name: M:SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr.#ctor(System.IntPtr[])
member.param: A chain of pointers to resolve

member.name: M:SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr.#ctor(System.Int32[])
member.param: A chain of pointers to resolve

member.name: M:SimpleMem.MultiLevelPtr.#ctor(System.Int64[])
member.param: A chain of pointers to resolve
